raga.name: Bhoopali
raga.description: A bright pentatonic raga with a serene devotional character. It omits Ma and Ni entirely, producing a clean, open sound.

forbidden.name: Shuddh Madhyam
forbidden.reason: Bhoopali is pentatonic and omits Ma entirely.

forbidden.name: Tivra Madhyam
forbidden.reason: Bhoopali omits Ma in all forms.

movement.aroha: S R G P D S'
movement.avaroha: S' D P G R S
movement.pakad_string: G R G P D, D P G R S

characteristic_phrases.context: Clean ascent without Ma or Ni
Resolution through the open pentatonic descent

note_rules.Pentatonic_purity: Avoid any Ma or Ni inflections; the raga depends on pentatonic clarity
note_rules.Ga_role: Ga is often emphasized and used to pivot between lower and upper tetrachords

vadi.role: King note — the bright center of Bhoopali. Often sustained in alaap.

samvadi.role: Queen note — supports Ga and anchors the upper tetrachord.

context.aesthetic: Clear, open pentatonic sound with a glowing evening character.

western_equivalent.relationship: Directly maps to the major pentatonic scale; Bhoopali's phrasing makes it unmistakably Indian.

lofi.notes: Pentatonic clarity blends well with mellow beats and simple harmonic beds.

ambient.notes: Open intervals and lack of half-steps make Bhoopali ideal for ambient textures.

calm.notes: Clean melodic lines suit meditation, yoga, and quiet focus.

upbeat.notes: Pentatonic structure can support upbeat rhythms if phrasing is kept crisp.

jazz_fusion.notes: Major pentatonic simplicity allows open jazz improvisation.

cinematic.notes: Bright serenity for hopeful or pastoral scenes.

downtempo.notes: Excellent: pentatonic motifs loop cleanly and stay singable over organic drums.

electronic.notes: Very strong: pentatonic hooks survive synth treatment and quantization, making it ideal for arps and toplines.

chillhop.notes: Bright clean melodies perfect with vinyl warmth.

reggae_dub.notes: Good fit: offbeat skanks + pentatonic lead feels natural; keep the bass on stable tones.

trap.notes: Excellent for catchy hooks; pentatonic contour reads instantly even over dense drums.

rock.notes: Excellent: maps well to guitar and riff logic; easy to keep melodic identity even at higher tempos.

fusion_notes: Clean pentatonic (no Ma, no Ni) offers open intervals
Avoid turning the melody into a generic major pentatonic riff: keep raga-like cadence shapes and ornamented turns.
Be cautious with resting emphasis: if you over-stress Dha as a landing tone, it can drift toward close neighbors.
Avoid 'extra' grace notes (especially the missing Ma/Ni) unless you intentionally want the sound of a related raga.
